_comment: Medicare Levy Surcharge (MLS) — FY 2025-26 thresholds and rates

singleThresholds.label: Tier 1
singleThresholds.description: 1% of taxable income for MLS purposes

singleThresholds.label: Tier 2
singleThresholds.description: 1.25% of taxable income for MLS purposes

singleThresholds.label: Tier 3
singleThresholds.description: 1.5% of taxable income for MLS purposes

familyThresholds.label: Tier 1
familyThresholds.description: 1% of taxable income for MLS purposes

familyThresholds.label: Tier 2
familyThresholds.description: 1.25% of taxable income for MLS purposes

familyThresholds.label: Tier 3
familyThresholds.description: 1.5% of taxable income for MLS purposes

familyThresholdRules.description: Family income threshold is increased by $1,500 for each MLS dependent child after the first child

incomeDefinition.description: Income for MLS purposes is the sum of the following components

components.name: Taxable income
components.description: Your taxable income (including net family trust distribution tax amounts; excluding FHSS scheme releases)

components.name: Reportable fringe benefits
components.description: Total reportable fringe benefits amounts (from your employer)

components.name: Total net investment losses
components.description: Financial investment losses plus rental property losses (negative gearing)

components.name: Reportable super contributions
components.description: Employer contributions plus deductible personal super contributions exceeding the standard amount

components.name: Spouse's trust net income
components.description: Net income of a trust attributable to your spouse (under section 98 of the Income Tax Assessment Act 1936). Less common component.

components.name: Exempt foreign employment income
components.description: Exempt foreign employment income (applies only if taxable income is $1 or more). Less common component.

incomeDefinition.note: Most people only need to consider the first 4 components. Components 5 and 6 apply in less common circumstances.

appropriateCover.description: To avoid MLS, you must hold 'appropriate level of private patient hospital cover'
requirements: Must be private patient hospital cover (not extras/general cover only)
Must be from a registered Australian health insurer
Must cover hospital treatment in Australia
Must cover yourself, your spouse, and all your dependants

excessLimits.description: Policy excess must not exceed $750 for singles or $1,500 for couples/families

doesNotCount: General (extras) cover only — dental, optical, physio, etc.
Cover from an overseas health fund

calculationRules.mlsAppliedTo: Income for MLS purposes (not just taxable income)
calculationRules.assessmentPeriod: Annual (1 July to 30 June), pro-rated by number of days without appropriate cover
calculationRules.proRataRule: If you had cover for part of the year, MLS is calculated on the number of days you did NOT have appropriate cover

lowIncomeSpouseRule.description: If you had a spouse for the full year and your own income for MLS purposes was $27,222 or less, you do not pay MLS even if family income exceeds the threshold (FY 2024-25 figure — FY 2025-26 figure to be confirmed)

workedExamples.name: Tom — Single, Tier 1 (ATO official example, FY 2025-26)

previousYear.note: Thresholds increased for 2025-26. The same $117,000 income was Tier 2 (1.25%) in 2024-25 but Tier 1 (1%) in 2025-26.
